revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chausuyama Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:茶臼山古墳 (壬生町)]]
{{Short description|Historic burial ground|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.489472|N|139.778111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu, Tochigi|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Chausuyama Kofun'''|茶臼山古墳 ||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the town of {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1202834|lt=Shimotsuga District|1=Shimotsuga District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1958.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/484 |title=茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Chausuyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> It is also sometimes referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Hanyu Chausuyama Kofun'''|羽生田茶臼山古墳|||qq=}} to distinguish it from many other ''kofun'' named "Chausuyama" all over Japan. ==Overview== The Chausuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|[[Zenpokoenfun]]|前方後円墳|3=|4=|5=|6=|7=|lt=|comement=|comment=|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at an elevation of about 100 meters above sea level in former Hanyu Village, on the left bank of the Kurokawa River, surrounded by a golf course to the north and east. Te surrounding area contains several other large tumuli from the same time period. The Chausuyama Kofun has an overall length of 91 meters, height of 12 meters, and was built in two-tiers. It was once encircled by a moat. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered on top of the Kofun, which when reconstructed proved to be a very large (180 cm) model of a house. Fragments of other ''haniwa'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery were also discovered during an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] conducted in 1895; however, the interior of the tumulus has not been excavated. From the shape of the tumulus and the types of ''haniwa'', the tumulus is believed to date from the second half of the 6th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The ''kofun'' is located about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|qid=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station|1=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|qid=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line|1=Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan|qq=}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== * [https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5060/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
